title: referrals — published macros
description: Three request-level macros surface referral context from the RequestContext (set by the referrals.resolve_active_referral middleware for admin write paths + inferred from the signed referral cookie for the public claim path). Registered by ReferralsServiceProvider::boot(). No Blueprint macros — the referrals-owned tables use standard column shapes.
namespace_note: Macros extend Illuminate\Http\Request. Middleware-set context bleeds through the Request accessor without needing to plumb it through service constructors.

macros.name: Request::activeReferralProgram
macros.adds: A single accessor on the framework Request class that returns the resolved active ReferralProgram model (or null when no program context).
macros.description: Reads from RequestContext::referralProgram() set by the referrals.enforce_active_program middleware. Lazy-loads the model on first access — subsequent calls in the same request return the cached instance.

macros.name: Request::activeReferralCode
macros.adds: Accessor returning the resolved ReferralCode for the current claim path.
macros.description: Set by the /claim/{code} route handler. Available on the request throughout the claim + resulting Referral write. Reads from cache (ref:code:{code}) when possible.

macros.name: Request::inboundReferral
macros.adds: Accessor returning the ReferralCode-derived Referral row when the caller carries a valid signed referral cookie (from a prior /claim/{code} call).
macros.description: Used on the signup + trigger paths to correlate the current user's action with a previously-claimed referral. Null when no cookie is present OR the referral is not in a state that can be transitioned by the current action.

no_blueprint_macros.reason: The referrals-owned tables (referral_programs, referral_codes, referrals, referral_rewards, referral_fraud_flags) use standard column shapes — no repeated JSONB blobs or shared column families that would justify a Blueprint macro.
